36: ## Installation
37: 
38: Install the gem and add to the application's Gemfile by executing:
39: 
40:     bundle add langchainrb
41: 
42: If bundler is not being used to manage dependencies, install the gem by executing:
43: 
44:     gem install langchainrb
45: 
46: Additional gems may be required. They're not included by default so you can include only what you need.
47: 
48: ## Usage
49: 
50: ```ruby
51: require "langchain"
52: ```
53: 
54: # Unified Interface for LLMs
55: 
56: The `Langchain::LLM` module provides a unified interface for interacting with various Large Language Model (LLM) providers. This abstraction allows you to easily switch between different LLM backends without changing your application code.
57: 
58: ## Supported LLM Providers
59: 
60: - Anthropic
61: - AWS Bedrock
62: - Azure OpenAI
63: - Cohere
64: - Google Gemini
65: - Google Vertex AI
66: - HuggingFace
67: - Mistral AI
68: - Ollama
69: - OpenAI
70: - Replicate
71: 
72: ## Usage
73: 
74: All LLM classes inherit from `Langchain::LLM::Base` and provide a consistent interface for common operations:
75: 
76: 1. Generating embeddings
77: 2. Generating prompt completions
78: 3. Generating chat completions
79: 
80: ### Initialization
81: 
82: Most LLM classes can be initialized with an API key and optional default options:
83: 
84: ```ruby
85: llm = Langchain::LLM::OpenAI.new(
86:   api_key: ENV["OPENAI_API_KEY"],
87:   default_options: { temperature: 0.7, chat_model: "gpt-4o" }
88: )
89: ```
90: 
91: ### Generating Embeddings
92: 
93: Use the `embed` method to generate embeddings for given text:
94: 
95: ```ruby
96: response = llm.embed(text: "Hello, world!")
97: embedding = response.embedding
98: ```
99: 
100: #### Accepted parameters for `embed()`
101: 
102: - `text`: (Required) The input text to embed.
103: - `model`: (Optional) The model name to use or default embedding model will be used.
104: 
105: ### Prompt completions
106: 
107: Use the `complete` method to generate completions for a given prompt:
108: 
109: ```ruby
110: response = llm.complete(prompt: "Once upon a time")
111: completion = response.completion
112: ```
113: 
114: #### Accepted parameters for `complete()`
115: 
116: - `prompt`: (Required) The input prompt for completion.
117: - `max_tokens`: (Optional) The maximum number of tokens to generate.
118: - `temperature`: (Optional) Controls randomness in generation. Higher values (e.g., 0.8) make output more random, while lower values (e.g., 0.2) make it more deterministic.
119: - `top_p`: (Optional) An alternative to temperature, controls diversity of generated tokens.
120: - `n`: (Optional) Number of completions to generate for each prompt.
121: - `stop`: (Optional) Sequences where the API will stop generating further tokens.
122: - `presence_penalty`: (Optional) Penalizes new tokens based on their presence in the text so far.
123: - `frequency_penalty`: (Optional) Penalizes new tokens based on their frequency in the text so far.
124: 
125: ### Generating Chat Completions
126: 
127: Use the `chat` method to generate chat completions:
128: 
129: ```ruby
130: messages = [
131:   { role: "system", content: "You are a helpful assistant." },
132:   { role: "user", content: "What's the weather like today?" }
133:   # Google Gemini and Google VertexAI expect messages in a different format:
134:   # { role: "user", parts: [{ text: "why is the sky blue?" }]}
135: ]
136: response = llm.chat(messages: messages)
137: chat_completion = response.chat_completion
138: ```
139: 
140: #### Accepted parameters for `chat()`
141: 
142: - `messages`: (Required) An array of message objects representing the conversation history.
143: - `model`: (Optional) The specific chat model to use.
144: - `temperature`: (Optional) Controls randomness in generation.
145: - `top_p`: (Optional) An alternative to temperature, controls diversity of generated tokens.
146: - `n`: (Optional) Number of chat completion choices to generate.
147: - `max_tokens`: (Optional) The maximum number of tokens to generate in the chat completion.
148: - `stop`: (Optional) Sequences where the API will stop generating further tokens.
149: - `presence_penalty`: (Optional) Penalizes new tokens based on their presence in the text so far.
150: - `frequency_penalty`: (Optional) Penalizes new tokens based on their frequency in the text so far.
151: - `logit_bias`: (Optional) Modifies the likelihood of specified tokens appearing in the completion.
152: - `user`: (Optional) A unique identifier representing your end-user.
153: - `tools`: (Optional) A list of tools the model may call.
154: - `tool_choice`: (Optional) Controls how the model calls functions.
155: 
156: ## Switching LLM Providers
157: 
158: Thanks to the unified interface, you can easily switch between different LLM providers by changing the class you instantiate:
159: 
160: ```ruby
161: # Using Anthropic
162: anthropic_llm = Langchain::LLM::Anthropic.new(api_key: ENV["ANTHROPIC_API_KEY"])
163: 
164: # Using Google Gemini
165: gemini_llm = Langchain::LLM::GoogleGemini.new(api_key: ENV["GOOGLE_GEMINI_API_KEY"])
166: 
167: # Using OpenAI
168: openai_llm = Langchain::LLM::OpenAI.new(api_key: ENV["OPENAI_API_KEY"])
169: ```
170: 
171: ## Response Objects
172: 
173: Each LLM method returns a response object that provides a consistent interface for accessing the results:
174: 
175: - `embedding`: Returns the embedding vector
176: - `completion`: Returns the generated text completion
177: - `chat_completion`: Returns the generated chat completion
178: - `tool_calls`: Returns tool calls made by the LLM
179: - `prompt_tokens`: Returns the number of tokens in the prompt
180: - `completion_tokens`: Returns the number of tokens in the completion
181: - `total_tokens`: Returns the total number of tokens used
182: 
183: > [!NOTE]
184: > While the core interface is consistent across providers, some LLMs may offer additional features or parameters. Consult the documentation for each LLM class to learn about provider-specific capabilities and options.
185: 
186: ### Prompt Management
187: 
188: #### Prompt Templates
189: 
190: Create a prompt with input variables:
191: 
192: ```ruby
193: prompt = Langchain::Prompt::PromptTemplate.new(template: "Tell me a {adjective} joke about {content}.", input_variables: ["adjective", "content"])
194: prompt.format(adjective: "funny", content: "chickens") # "Tell me a funny joke about chickens."
195: ```
196: 
197: Creating a PromptTemplate using just a prompt and no input_variables:
198: 
199: ```ruby
200: prompt = Langchain::Prompt::PromptTemplate.from_template("Tell me a funny joke about chickens.")
201: prompt.input_variables # []
202: prompt.format # "Tell me a funny joke about chickens."
203: ```
204: 
205: Save prompt template to JSON file:
206: 
207: ```ruby
208: prompt.save(file_path: "spec/fixtures/prompt/prompt_template.json")
209: ```
210: 
211: Loading a new prompt template using a JSON file:
212: 
213: ```ruby
214: prompt = Langchain::Prompt.load_from_path(file_path: "spec/fixtures/prompt/prompt_template.json")
215: prompt.input_variables # ["adjective", "content"]
216: ```
217: 
218: #### Few Shot Prompt Templates
219: 
220: Create a prompt with a few shot examples:
221: 
222: ```ruby
223: prompt = Langchain::Prompt::FewShotPromptTemplate.new(
224:   prefix: "Write antonyms for the following words.",
225:   suffix: "Input: {adjective}\nOutput:",
226:   example_prompt: Langchain::Prompt::PromptTemplate.new(
227:     input_variables: ["input", "output"],
228:     template: "Input: {input}\nOutput: {output}"
229:   ),
230:   examples: [
231:     { "input": "happy", "output": "sad" },
232:     { "input": "tall", "output": "short" }
233:   ],
234:    input_variables: ["adjective"]
235: )
236: 
237: prompt.format(adjective: "good")
238: 
239: # Write antonyms for the following words.
240: #
241: # Input: happy
242: # Output: sad
243: #
244: # Input: tall
245: # Output: short
246: #
247: # Input: good
248: # Output:
249: ```
250: 
251: Save prompt template to JSON file:
252: 
253: ```ruby
254: prompt.save(file_path: "spec/fixtures/prompt/few_shot_prompt_template.json")
255: ```
256: 
257: Loading a new prompt template using a JSON file:
258: 
259: ```ruby
260: prompt = Langchain::Prompt.load_from_path(file_path: "spec/fixtures/prompt/few_shot_prompt_template.json")
261: prompt.prefix # "Write antonyms for the following words."
262: ```
263: 
264: Loading a new prompt template using a YAML file:
265: 
266: ```ruby
267: prompt = Langchain::Prompt.load_from_path(file_path: "spec/fixtures/prompt/prompt_template.yaml")
268: prompt.input_variables #=> ["adjective", "content"]
269: ```
270: 
271: 
272: ### Output Parsers
273: 
274: Parse LLM text responses into structured output, such as JSON.
275: 
276: #### Structured Output Parser
277: 
278: You can use the `StructuredOutputParser` to generate a prompt that instructs the LLM to provide a JSON response adhering to a specific JSON schema:
279: 
280: ```ruby
281: json_schema = {
282:   type: "object",
283:   properties: {
284:     name: {
285:       type: "string",
286:       description: "Persons name"
287:     },
288:     age: {
289:       type: "number",
290:       description: "Persons age"
291:     },
292:     interests: {
293:       type: "array",
294:       items: {
295:         type: "object",
296:         properties: {
297:           interest: {
298:             type: "string",
299:             description: "A topic of interest"
300:           },
301:           levelOfInterest: {
302:             type: "number",
303:             description: "A value between 0 and 100 of how interested the person is in this interest"
304:           }
305:         },
306:         required: ["interest", "levelOfInterest"],
307:         additionalProperties: false
308:       },
309:       minItems: 1,
310:       maxItems: 3,
311:       description: "A list of the person's interests"
312:     }
313:   },
314:   required: ["name", "age", "interests"],
315:   additionalProperties: false
316: }
317: parser = Langchain::OutputParsers::StructuredOutputParser.from_json_schema(json_schema)
318: prompt = Langchain::Prompt::PromptTemplate.new(template: "Generate details of a fictional character.\n{format_instructions}\nCharacter description: {description}", input_variables: ["description", "format_instructions"])
319: prompt_text = prompt.format(description: "Korean chemistry student", format_instructions: parser.get_format_instructions)
320: # Generate details of a fictional character.
321: # You must format your output as a JSON value that adheres to a given "JSON Schema" instance.
322: # ...
323: ```
324: 
325: Then parse the llm response:
326: 
327: ```ruby
328: llm = Langchain::LLM::OpenAI.new(api_key: ENV["OPENAI_API_KEY"])
329: llm_response = llm.chat(messages: [{role: "user", content: prompt_text}]).completion
330: parser.parse(llm_response)
331: # {
332: #   "name" => "Kim Ji-hyun",
333: #   "age" => 22,
334: #   "interests" => [
335: #     {
336: #       "interest" => "Organic Chemistry",
337: #       "levelOfInterest" => 85
338: #     },
339: #     ...
340: #   ]
341: # }
342: ```
343: 
344: If the parser fails to parse the LLM response, you can use the `OutputFixingParser`. It sends an error message, prior output, and the original prompt text to the LLM, asking for a "fixed" response:
345: 
346: ```ruby
347: begin
348:   parser.parse(llm_response)
349: rescue Langchain::OutputParsers::OutputParserException => e
350:   fix_parser = Langchain::OutputParsers::OutputFixingParser.from_llm(
351:     llm: llm,
352:     parser: parser
353:   )
354:   fix_parser.parse(llm_response)
355: end
356: ```
357: 
358: Alternatively, if you don't need to handle the `OutputParserException`, you can simplify the code:
359: 
360: ```ruby
361: # we already have the `OutputFixingParser`:
362: # parser = Langchain::OutputParsers::StructuredOutputParser.from_json_schema(json_schema)
363: fix_parser = Langchain::OutputParsers::OutputFixingParser.from_llm(
364:   llm: llm,
365:   parser: parser
366: )
367: fix_parser.parse(llm_response)
368: ```
369: 
370: See [here](https://github.com/patterns-ai-core/langchainrb/tree/main/examples/create_and_manage_prompt_templates_using_structured_output_parser.rb) for a concrete example
371: 
372: ## Building Retrieval Augment Generation (RAG) system
373: RAG is a methodology that assists LLMs generate accurate and up-to-date information.
374: A typical RAG workflow follows the 3 steps below:
375: 1. Relevant knowledge (or data) is retrieved from the knowledge base (typically a vector search DB)
376: 2. A prompt, containing retrieved knowledge above, is constructed.
377: 3. LLM receives the prompt above to generate a text completion.
378: Most common use-case for a RAG system is powering Q&A systems where users pose natural language questions and receive answers in natural language.
379: 
380: ### Vector search databases
381: Langchain.rb provides a convenient unified interface on top of supported vectorsearch databases that make it easy to configure your index, add data, query and retrieve from it.
382: 
383: #### Supported vector search databases and features:
384: 
385: | Database                                                                                   | Open-source        | Cloud offering     |
386: | --------                                                                                   |:------------------:| :------------:     |
387: | [Chroma](https://trychroma.com/?utm_source=langchainrb&utm_medium=github)                  | ✅                 | ✅                 |
388: | [Hnswlib](https://github.com/nmslib/hnswlib/?utm_source=langchainrb&utm_medium=github)     | ✅                 | ❌                 |
389: | [Milvus](https://milvus.io/?utm_source=langchainrb&utm_medium=github)                      | ✅                 | ✅ Zilliz Cloud    |
390: | [Pinecone](https://www.pinecone.io/?utm_source=langchainrb&utm_medium=github)              | ❌                 | ✅                 |
391: | [Pgvector](https://github.com/pgvector/pgvector/?utm_source=langchainrb&utm_medium=github) | ✅                 | ✅                 |
392: | [Qdrant](https://qdrant.tech/?utm_source=langchainrb&utm_medium=github)                    | ✅                 | ✅                 |
393: | [Weaviate](https://weaviate.io/?utm_source=langchainrb&utm_medium=github)                  | ✅                 | ✅                 |
394: | [Elasticsearch](https://www.elastic.co/?utm_source=langchainrb&utm_medium=github)          | ✅                 | ✅                 |
395: 
396: ### Using Vector Search Databases 🔍
397: 
398: Pick the vector search database you'll be using, add the gem dependency and instantiate the client:
399: ```ruby
400: gem "weaviate-ruby", "~> 0.8.9"
401: ```
402: 
403: Choose and instantiate the LLM provider you'll be using to generate embeddings
404: ```ruby
405: llm = Langchain::LLM::OpenAI.new(api_key: ENV["OPENAI_API_KEY"])
406: ```
407: 
408: ```ruby
409: client = Langchain::Vectorsearch::Weaviate.new(
410:     url: ENV["WEAVIATE_URL"],
411:     api_key: ENV["WEAVIATE_API_KEY"],
412:     index_name: "Documents",
413:     llm: llm
414: )
415: ```
416: 
417: You can instantiate any other supported vector search database:
418: ```ruby
419: client = Langchain::Vectorsearch::Chroma.new(...)   # `gem "chroma-db", "~> 0.6.0"`
420: client = Langchain::Vectorsearch::Hnswlib.new(...)  # `gem "hnswlib", "~> 0.8.1"`
421: client = Langchain::Vectorsearch::Milvus.new(...)   # `gem "milvus", "~> 0.9.3"`
422: client = Langchain::Vectorsearch::Pinecone.new(...) # `gem "pinecone", "~> 1.0"`
423: client = Langchain::Vectorsearch::Pgvector.new(...) # `gem "pgvector", "~> 0.2"`
424: client = Langchain::Vectorsearch::Qdrant.new(...)   # `gem "qdrant-ruby", "~> 0.9.3"`
425: client = Langchain::Vectorsearch::Elasticsearch.new(...)   # `gem "elasticsearch", "~> 8.2.0"`
426: ```
427: 
428: Create the default schema:
429: ```ruby
430: client.create_default_schema
431: ```
432: 
433: Add plain text data to your vector search database:
434: ```ruby
435: client.add_texts(
436:   texts: [
437:     "Begin by preheating your oven to 375°F (190°C). Prepare four boneless, skinless chicken breasts by cutting a pocket into the side of each breast, being careful not to cut all the way through. Season the chicken with salt and pepper to taste. In a large skillet, melt 2 tablespoons of unsalted butter over medium heat. Add 1 small diced onion and 2 minced garlic cloves, and cook until softened, about 3-4 minutes. Add 8 ounces of fresh spinach and cook until wilted, about 3 minutes. Remove the skillet from heat and let the mixture cool slightly.",
438:       "In a bowl, combine the spinach mixture with 4 ounces of softened cream cheese, 1/4 cup of grated Parmesan cheese, 1/4 cup of shredded mozzarella cheese, and 1/4 teaspoon of red pepper flakes. Mix until well combined. Stuff each chicken breast pocket with an equal amount of the spinach mixture. Seal the pocket with a toothpick if necessary. In the same skillet, heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil over medium-high heat. Add the stuffed chicken breasts and sear on each side for 3-4 minutes, or until golden brown."
439:   ]
440: )
441: ```
442: 
443: Or use the file parsers to load, parse and index data into your database:
444: ```ruby
445: my_pdf = Langchain.root.join("path/to/my.pdf")
446: my_text = Langchain.root.join("path/to/my.txt")
447: my_docx = Langchain.root.join("path/to/my.docx")
448: 
449: client.add_data(paths: [my_pdf, my_text, my_docx])
450: ```
451: Supported file formats: docx, html, pdf, text, json, jsonl, csv, xlsx, eml, pptx.
452: 
453: Retrieve similar documents based on the query string passed in:
454: ```ruby
455: client.similarity_search(
456:   query:,
457:   k:       # number of results to be retrieved
458: )
459: ```
460: 
461: Retrieve similar documents based on the query string passed in via the [HyDE technique](https://arxiv.org/abs/2212.10496):
462: ```ruby
463: client.similarity_search_with_hyde()
464: ```
465: 
466: Retrieve similar documents based on the embedding passed in:
467: ```ruby
468: client.similarity_search_by_vector(
469:   embedding:,
470:   k:       # number of results to be retrieved
471: )
472: ```
473: 
474: RAG-based querying
475: ```ruby
476: client.ask(question: "...")
477: ```

479: ## Assistants
480: `Langchain::Assistant` is a powerful and flexible class that combines Large Language Models (LLMs), tools, and conversation management to create intelligent, interactive assistants. It's designed to handle complex conversations, execute tools, and provide coherent responses based on the context of the interaction.
481: 
482: ### Features
483: * Supports multiple LLM providers (OpenAI, Google Gemini, Anthropic, Mistral AI and open-source models via Ollama)
484: * Integrates with various tools to extend functionality
485: * Manages conversation threads
486: * Handles automatic and manual tool execution
487: * Supports different message formats for various LLM providers
488: 
489: ### Usage
490: ```ruby
491: llm = Langchain::LLM::OpenAI.new(api_key: ENV["OPENAI_API_KEY"])
492: assistant = Langchain::Assistant.new(
493:   llm: llm,
494:   instructions: "You're a helpful AI assistant",
495:   tools: [Langchain::Tool::NewsRetriever.new(api_key: ENV["NEWS_API_KEY"])]
496: )
497: 
498: # Add a user message and run the assistant
499: assistant.add_message_and_run!(content: "What's the latest news about AI?")
500: 
501: # Supply an image to the assistant
502: assistant.add_message_and_run!(
503:   content: "Describe this image.",
504:   image_url: "https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/dd/Gfp-wisconsin-madison-the-nature-boardwalk.jpg/2560px-Gfp-wisconsin-madison-the-nature-boardwalk.jpg"
505: )
506: 
507: # Access the conversation thread
508: messages = assistant.messages
509: 
510: # Run the assistant with automatic tool execution
511: assistant.run(auto_tool_execution: true)
512: 
513: # If you want to stream the response, you can add a response handler
514: assistant = Langchain::Assistant.new(
515:   llm: llm,
516:   instructions: "You're a helpful AI assistant",
517:   tools: [Langchain::Tool::NewsRetriever.new(api_key: ENV["NEWS_API_KEY"])]
518: ) do |response_chunk|
519:   # ...handle the response stream
520:   # print(response_chunk.inspect)
521: end
522: assistant.add_message(content: "Hello")
523: assistant.run(auto_tool_execution: true)
524: ```
525: 
526: Note that streaming is not currently supported for all LLMs.
527: 
528: ### Configuration
529: * `llm`: The LLM instance to use (required)
530: * `tools`: An array of tool instances (optional)
531: * `instructions`: System instructions for the assistant (optional)
532: * `tool_choice`: Specifies how tools should be selected. Default: "auto". A specific tool function name can be passed. This will force the Assistant to **always** use this function.
533: * `parallel_tool_calls`: Whether to make multiple parallel tool calls. Default: true
534: * `add_message_callback`: A callback function (proc, lambda) that is called when any message is added to the conversation (optional)
535: ```ruby
536: assistant.add_message_callback = -> (message) { puts "New message: #{message}" }
537: ```
538: * `tool_execution_callback`: A callback function (proc, lambda) that is called right before a tool is executed (optional)
539: ```ruby
540: assistant.tool_execution_callback = -> (tool_call_id, tool_name, method_name, tool_arguments) { puts "Executing tool_call_id: #{tool_call_id}, tool_name: #{tool_name}, method_name: #{method_name}, tool_arguments: #{tool_arguments}" }
541: ```
542: 
543: ### Key Methods
544: * `add_message`: Adds a user message to the messages array
545: * `run!`: Processes the conversation and generates responses
546: * `add_message_and_run!`: Combines adding a message and running the assistant
547: * `submit_tool_output`: Manually submit output to a tool call
548: * `messages`: Returns a list of ongoing messages
549: 
550: ### Built-in Tools 🛠️
551: * `Langchain::Tool::Calculator`: Useful for evaluating math expressions. Requires `gem "eqn"`.
552: * `Langchain::Tool::Database`: Connect your SQL database. Requires `gem "sequel"`.
553: * `Langchain::Tool::FileSystem`: Interact with the file system (read & write).
554: * `Langchain::Tool::GoogleSearch`: Wrapper around SerpApi's Google Search API. Requires `gem "google_search_results"`.
555: * `Langchain::Tool::NewsRetriever`: A wrapper around [NewsApi.org](https://newsapi.org) to fetch news articles.
556: * `Langchain::Tool::RubyCodeInterpreter`: Useful for evaluating generated Ruby code. Requires `gem "safe_ruby"` (In need of a better solution).
557: * `Langchain::Tool::Tavily`: A wrapper around [Tavily AI](https://tavily.com).
558: * `Langchain::Tool::Vectorsearch`: A wrapper for vector search classes.
559: * `Langchain::Tool::Weather`: Calls [Open Weather API](https://home.openweathermap.org) to retrieve the current weather.
560: * `Langchain::Tool::Wikipedia`: Calls Wikipedia API. Requires `gem "wikipedia-client"`.
561: 
562: ### Creating custom Tools
563: The Langchain::Assistant can be easily extended with custom tools by creating classes that `extend Langchain::ToolDefinition` module and implement required methods.
564: ```ruby
565: class MovieInfoTool
566:   extend Langchain::ToolDefinition
567: 
568:   define_function :search_movie, description: "MovieInfoTool: Search for a movie by title" do
569:     property :query, type: "string", description: "The movie title to search for", required: true
570:   end
571: 
572:   define_function :get_movie_details, description: "MovieInfoTool: Get detailed information about a specific movie" do
573:     property :movie_id, type: "integer", description: "The TMDb ID of the movie", required: true
574:   end
575: 
576:   def initialize(api_key:)
577:     @api_key = api_key
578:   end
579: 
580:   def search_movie(query:)
581:     tool_response(...)
582:   end
583: 
584:   def get_movie_details(movie_id:)
585:     tool_response(...)
586:   end
587: end
588: ```
589: 
590: #### Example usage:
591: ```ruby
592: movie_tool = MovieInfoTool.new(api_key: "...")
593: 
594: assistant = Langchain::Assistant.new(
595:   llm: llm,
596:   instructions: "You're a helpful AI assistant that can provide movie information",
597:   tools: [movie_tool]
598: )
599: 
600: assistant.add_message_and_run(content: "Can you tell me about the movie 'Inception'?")
601: # Check the response in the last message in the conversation
602: assistant.messages.last
603: ```
604: 
605: ### Error Handling
606: The assistant includes error handling for invalid inputs, unsupported LLM types, and tool execution failures. It uses a state machine to manage the conversation flow and handle different scenarios gracefully.
607: 
608: ### Demos
609: 1. [Building an AI Assistant that operates a simulated E-commerce Store](https://www.loom.com/share/83aa4fd8dccb492aad4ca95da40ed0b2)
610: 2. [New Langchain.rb Assistants interface](https://www.loom.com/share/e883a4a49b8746c1b0acf9d58cf6da36)
611: 3. [Langchain.rb Assistant demo with NewsRetriever and function calling on Gemini](https://youtu.be/-ieyahrpDpM&t=1477s) - [code](https://github.com/palladius/gemini-news-crawler)
612: 
613: ## Evaluations (Evals)
614: The Evaluations module is a collection of tools that can be used to evaluate and track the performance of the output products by LLM and your RAG (Retrieval Augmented Generation) pipelines.
615: 
616: ### RAGAS
617: Ragas helps you evaluate your Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) pipelines. The implementation is based on this [paper](https://arxiv.org/abs/2309.15217) and the original Python [repo](https://github.com/explodinggradients/ragas). Ragas tracks the following 3 metrics and assigns the 0.0 - 1.0 scores:
618: * Faithfulness - the answer is grounded in the given context.
619: * Context Relevance - the retrieved context is focused, containing little to no irrelevant information.
620: * Answer Relevance - the generated answer addresses the actual question that was provided.
621: 
622: ```ruby
623: # We recommend using Langchain::LLM::OpenAI as your llm for Ragas
624: ragas = Langchain::Evals::Ragas::Main.new(llm: llm)
625: 
626: # The answer that the LLM generated
627: # The question (or the original prompt) that was asked
628: # The context that was retrieved (usually from a vectorsearch database)
629: ragas.score(answer: "", question: "", context: "")
630: # =>
631: # {
632: #   ragas_score: 0.6601257446503674,
633: #   answer_relevance_score: 0.9573145866787608,
634: #   context_relevance_score: 0.6666666666666666,
635: #   faithfulness_score: 0.5
636: # }
637: ```
638: 
639: ## Examples
640: Additional examples available: [/examples](https://github.com/patterns-ai-core/langchainrb/tree/main/examples)
641: 
642: ## Logging
643: 
644: Langchain.rb uses the standard Ruby [Logger](https://ruby-doc.org/stdlib-2.4.0/libdoc/logger/rdoc/Logger.html) mechanism and defaults to same `level` value (currently `Logger::DEBUG`).
645: 
646: To show all log messages:
647: 
648: ```ruby
649: Langchain.logger.level = Logger::DEBUG
650: ```
651: 
652: The logger logs to `STDOUT` by default. In order to configure the log destination (ie. log to a file) do:
653: 
654: ```ruby
655: Langchain.logger = Logger.new("path/to/file", **Langchain::LOGGER_OPTIONS)
656: ```
657: 
658: ## Problems
659: If you're having issues installing `unicode` gem required by `pragmatic_segmenter`, try running:
660: ```bash
661: gem install unicode -- --with-cflags="-Wno-incompatible-function-pointer-types"
662: ```
663: 
664: ## Development
665: 
666: 1. `git clone https://github.com/patterns-ai-core/langchainrb.git`
667: 2. `cp .env.example .env`, then fill out the environment variables in `.env`
668: 3. `bundle exec rake` to ensure that the tests pass and to run standardrb
669: 4. `bin/console` to load the gem in a REPL session. Feel free to add your own instances of LLMs, Tools, Agents, etc. and experiment with them.
670: 5. Optionally, install lefthook git hooks for pre-commit to auto lint: `gem install lefthook && lefthook install -f`
671: 
672: ## Discord
673: Join us in the [Langchain.rb](https://discord.gg/WDARp7J2n8) Discord server.
674: 
675: ## Star History
676: 
677: [![Star History Chart](https://api.star-history.com/svg?repos=andreibondarev/langchainrb&type=Date)](https://star-history.com/#andreibondarev/langchainrb&Date)
678: 
679: ## Contributing
680: 
681: Bug reports and pull requests are welcome on GitHub at https://github.com/patterns-ai-core/langchainrb.
682: 
683: ## License

685: The gem is available as open source under the terms of the [MIT License](https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT).

### Step 1: Orient in the codebase

Langchain.rb is a Ruby framework for LLM-powered apps, with a unified `Langchain::LLM::*` client family for providers, `Langchain::Assistant` for tool-calling conversations, and an adapter layer in between.

Key locations:

- Main entry point: `lib/langchain.rb`
- Assistant: `lib/langchain/assistant.rb`
- Adapter selector: `lib/langchain/assistant/llm/adapter.rb`
- Per-provider assistant adapters: `lib/langchain/assistant/llm/adapters/*.rb`
- Per-provider assistant message forms: `lib/langchain/assistant/messages/*.rb`
- Shared adapter base: `lib/langchain/assistant/llm/adapters/base.rb`
- Shared message base: `lib/langchain/assistant/messages/base.rb`
- LLM client base: `lib/langchain/llm/base.rb`
- Per-provider LLM clients: `lib/langchain/llm/*.rb` (e.g. `openai.rb`, `anthropic.rb`, `google_gemini.rb`, `ollama.rb`, `mistralai.rb`)
- Shared response base: `lib/langchain/llm/response/base_response.rb`
- Per-provider response wrappers: `lib/langchain/llm/response/*.rb`
- Tool definition mixin: `lib/langchain/tool_definition.rb`
- Tool response wrapper: `lib/langchain/tool_response.rb`

---

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Langchain
4:   # Assistants are Agent-like objects that leverage helpful instructions, LLMs, tools and knowledge to respond to user queries.
5:   # Assistants can be configured with an LLM of your choice, any vector search database and easily extended with additional tools.
6:   #
7:   # Usage:
8:   #     llm = Langchain::LLM::GoogleGemini.new(api_key: ENV["GOOGLE_GEMINI_API_KEY"])
9:   #     assistant = Langchain::Assistant.new(
10:   #       llm: llm,
11:   #       instructions: "You're a News Reporter AI",
12:   #       tools: [Langchain::Tool::NewsRetriever.new(api_key: ENV["NEWS_API_KEY"])]
13:   #     )
14:   class Assistant
15:     attr_reader :llm,
16:       :instructions,
17:       :state,
18:       :llm_adapter,
19:       :messages,
20:       :tool_choice,
21:       :total_prompt_tokens,
22:       :total_completion_tokens,
23:       :total_tokens
24: 
25:     attr_accessor :tools,
26:       :add_message_callback,
27:       :tool_execution_callback,
28:       :parallel_tool_calls
29: 
30:     # Create a new assistant
31:     #
32:     # @param llm [Langchain::LLM::Base] LLM instance that the assistant will use
33:     # @param tools [Array<Langchain::Tool::Base>] Tools that the assistant has access to
34:     # @param instructions [String] The system instructions
35:     # @param tool_choice [String] Specify how tools should be selected. Options: "auto", "any", "none", or <specific function name>
36:     # @param parallel_tool_calls [Boolean] Whether or not to run tools in parallel
37:     # @param messages [Array<Langchain::Assistant::Messages::Base>] The messages
38:     # @param add_message_callback [Proc] A callback function (Proc or lambda) that is called when any message is added to the conversation
39:     # @param tool_execution_callback [Proc] A callback function (Proc or lambda) that is called right before a tool function is executed
40:     def initialize(
41:       llm:,
42:       tools: [],
43:       instructions: nil,
44:       tool_choice: "auto",
45:       parallel_tool_calls: true,
46:       messages: [],
47:       # Callbacks
48:       add_message_callback: nil,
49:       tool_execution_callback: nil,
50:       &block
51:     )
52:       unless tools.is_a?(Array) && tools.all? { |tool| tool.class.singleton_class.include?(Langchain::ToolDefinition) }
53:         raise ArgumentError, "Tools must be an array of objects extending Langchain::ToolDefinition"
54:       end
55: 
56:       @llm = llm
57:       @llm_adapter = LLM::Adapter.build(llm)
58: 
59:       @add_message_callback = add_message_callback if validate_callback!("add_message_callback", add_message_callback)
60:       @tool_execution_callback = tool_execution_callback if validate_callback!("tool_execution_callback", tool_execution_callback)
61: 
62:       self.messages = messages
63:       @tools = tools
64:       @parallel_tool_calls = parallel_tool_calls
65:       self.tool_choice = tool_choice
66:       self.instructions = instructions
67:       @block = block
68:       @state = :ready
69: 
70:       @total_prompt_tokens = 0
71:       @total_completion_tokens = 0
72:       @total_tokens = 0
73:     end
74: 
75:     # Add a user message to the messages array
76:     #
77:     # @param role [String] The role attribute of the message. Default: "user"
78:     # @param content [String] The content of the message
79:     # @param image_url [String] The URL of the image to include in the message
80:     # @param tool_calls [Array<Hash>] The tool calls to include in the message
81:     # @param tool_call_id [String] The ID of the tool call to include in the message
82:     # @return [Array<Langchain::Message>] The messages
83:     def add_message(role: "user", content: nil, image_url: nil, tool_calls: [], tool_call_id: nil)
84:       message = build_message(role: role, content: content, image_url: image_url, tool_calls: tool_calls, tool_call_id: tool_call_id)
85: 
86:       # Call the callback with the message
87:       add_message_callback.call(message) if add_message_callback # rubocop:disable Style/SafeNavigation
88: 
89:       # Prepend the message to the messages array
90:       messages << message
91: 
92:       @state = :ready
93: 
94:       messages
95:     end
96: 
97:     # Convert messages to an LLM APIs-compatible array of hashes
98:     #
99:     # @return [Array<Hash>] Messages as an OpenAI API-compatible array of hashes
100:     def array_of_message_hashes
101:       messages
102:         .map(&:to_hash)
103:         .compact
104:     end
105: 
106:     # Only used by the Assistant when it calls the LLM#complete() method
107:     def prompt_of_concatenated_messages
108:       messages.map(&:to_s).join
109:     end
110: 
111:     # Set multiple messages
112:     #
113:     # @param messages [Array<Langchain::Message>] The messages to set
114:     # @return [Array<Langchain::Message>] The messages
115:     def messages=(messages)
116:       raise ArgumentError, "messages array must only contain Langchain::Message instance(s)" unless messages.is_a?(Array) && messages.all? { |m| m.is_a?(Messages::Base) }
117: 
118:       @messages = messages
119:     end
120: 
121:     # Add multiple messages
122:     #
123:     # @param messages [Array<Hash>] The messages to add
124:     # @return [Array<Langchain::Message>] The messages
125:     def add_messages(messages:)
126:       messages.each do |message_hash|
127:         add_message(**message_hash.slice(:content, :role, :tool_calls, :tool_call_id))
128:       end
129:     end
130: 
131:     # Run the assistant
132:     #
133:     # @param auto_tool_execution [Boolean] Whether or not to automatically run tools
134:     # @return [Array<Langchain::Message>] The messages
135:     def run(auto_tool_execution: false)
136:       if messages.empty?
137:         Langchain.logger.warn("#{self.class} - No messages to process")
138:         @state = :completed
139:         return
140:       end
141: 
142:       @state = :in_progress
143:       @state = handle_state until run_finished?(auto_tool_execution)
144: 
145:       messages
146:     end
147: 
148:     # Run the assistant with automatic tool execution
149:     #
150:     # @return [Array<Langchain::Message>] The messages
151:     def run!
152:       run(auto_tool_execution: true)
153:     end
154: 
155:     # Add a user message and run the assistant
156:     #
157:     # @param content [String] The content of the message
158:     # @param auto_tool_execution [Boolean] Whether or not to automatically run tools
159:     # @return [Array<Langchain::Message>] The messages
160:     def add_message_and_run(content: nil, image_url: nil, auto_tool_execution: false)
161:       add_message(content: content, image_url: image_url, role: "user")
162:       run(auto_tool_execution: auto_tool_execution)
163:     end
164: 
165:     # Add a user message and run the assistant with automatic tool execution
166:     #
167:     # @param content [String] The content of the message
168:     # @return [Array<Langchain::Message>] The messages
169:     def add_message_and_run!(content: nil, image_url: nil)
170:       add_message_and_run(content: content, image_url: image_url, auto_tool_execution: true)
171:     end
172: 
173:     # Submit tool output
174:     #
175:     # @param tool_call_id [String] The ID of the tool call to submit output for
176:     # @param output [String] The output of the tool
177:     # @return [Array<Langchain::Message>] The messages
178:     def submit_tool_output(tool_call_id:, output:)
179:       # TODO: Validate that `tool_call_id` is valid by scanning messages and checking if this tool call ID was invoked
180:       add_message(role: @llm_adapter.tool_role, content: output, tool_call_id: tool_call_id)
181:     end
182: 
183:     # Delete all messages
184:     #
185:     # @return [Array] Empty messages array
186:     def clear_messages!
187:       # TODO: If this a bug? Should we keep the "system" message?
188:       @messages = []
189:     end
190: 
191:     # Set new instructions
192:     #
193:     # @param new_instructions [String] New instructions that will be set as a system message
194:     # @return [Array<Langchain::Message>] The messages
195:     def instructions=(new_instructions)
196:       @instructions = new_instructions
197: 
198:       if @llm_adapter.support_system_message?
199:         # TODO: Should we still set a system message even if @instructions is "" or nil?
200:         replace_system_message!(content: new_instructions)
201:       end
202:     end
203: 
204:     # Set tool_choice, how tools should be selected
205:     #
206:     # @param new_tool_choice [String] Tool choice
207:     # @return [String] Selected tool choice
208:     def tool_choice=(new_tool_choice)
209:       validate_tool_choice!(new_tool_choice)
210:       @tool_choice = new_tool_choice
211:     end
212: 
213:     private
214: 
215:     # Replace old system message with new one
216:     #
217:     # @param content [String] New system message content
218:     # @return [Array<Langchain::Message>] The messages
219:     def replace_system_message!(content:)
220:       messages.delete_if(&:system?)
221:       return if content.nil?
222: 
223:       message = build_message(role: "system", content: content)
224:       messages.unshift(message)
225:     end
226: 
227:     # TODO: If tool_choice = "tool_function_name" and then tool is removed from the assistant, should we set tool_choice back to "auto"?
228:     def validate_tool_choice!(tool_choice)
229:       allowed_tool_choices = llm_adapter.allowed_tool_choices.concat(available_tool_names)
230:       unless allowed_tool_choices.include?(tool_choice)
231:         raise ArgumentError, "Tool choice must be one of: #{allowed_tool_choices.join(", ")}"
232:       end
233:     end
234: 
235:     # Check if the run is finished
236:     #
237:     # @param auto_tool_execution [Boolean] Whether or not to automatically run tools
238:     # @return [Boolean] Whether the run is finished
239:     def run_finished?(auto_tool_execution)
240:       finished_states = [:completed, :failed]
241: 
242:       requires_manual_action = (@state == :requires_action) && !auto_tool_execution
243:       finished_states.include?(@state) || requires_manual_action
244:     end
245: 
246:     # Handle the current state and transition to the next state
247:     #
248:     # @return [Symbol] The next state
249:     def handle_state
250:       case @state
251:       when :in_progress
252:         process_latest_message
253:       when :requires_action
254:         execute_tools
255:       end
256:     end
257: 
258:     # Process the latest message
259:     #
260:     # @return [Symbol] The next state
261:     def process_latest_message
262:       last_message = messages.last
263: 
264:       case last_message.standard_role
265:       when :system
266:         handle_system_message
267:       when :llm
268:         handle_llm_message
269:       when :user, :tool
270:         handle_user_or_tool_message
271:       else
272:         handle_unexpected_message
273:       end
274:     end
275: 
276:     # Handle system message scenario
277:     #
278:     # @return [Symbol] The completed state
279:     def handle_system_message
280:       Langchain.logger.warn("#{self.class} - At least one user message is required after a system message")
281:       :completed
282:     end
283: 
284:     # Handle LLM message scenario
285:     #
286:     # @return [Symbol] The next state
287:     def handle_llm_message
288:       messages.last.tool_calls.any? ? :requires_action : :completed
289:     end
290: 
291:     # Handle unexpected message scenario
292:     #
293:     # @return [Symbol] The failed state
294:     def handle_unexpected_message
295:       Langchain.logger.error("#{self.class} - Unexpected message role encountered: #{messages.last.standard_role}")
296:       :failed
297:     end
298: 
299:     # Handle user or tool message scenario by processing the LLM response
300:     #
301:     # @return [Symbol] The next state
302:     def handle_user_or_tool_message
303:       response = chat_with_llm
304: 
305:       add_message(role: response.role, content: response.chat_completion, tool_calls: response.tool_calls)
306:       record_used_tokens(response.prompt_tokens, response.completion_tokens, response.total_tokens)
307: 
308:       set_state_for(response: response)
309:     end
310: 
311:     def set_state_for(response:)
312:       if response.tool_calls.any?
313:         :in_progress
314:       elsif response.chat_completion
315:         :completed
316:       elsif response.completion # Currently only used by Ollama
317:         :completed
318:       else
319:         Langchain.logger.error("#{self.class} - LLM response does not contain tool calls, chat or completion response")
320:         :failed
321:       end
322:     end
323: 
324:     # Execute the tools based on the tool calls in the last message
325:     #
326:     # @return [Symbol] The next state
327:     def execute_tools
328:       run_tools(messages.last.tool_calls)
329:       :in_progress
330:     rescue => e
331:       Langchain.logger.error("#{self.class} - Error running tools: #{e.message}; #{e.backtrace.join('\n')}")
332:       :failed
333:     end
334: 
335:     # Call to the LLM#chat() method
336:     #
337:     # @return [Langchain::LLM::BaseResponse] The LLM response object
338:     def chat_with_llm
339:       Langchain.logger.debug("#{self.class} - Sending a call to #{llm.class}")
340: 
341:       params = @llm_adapter.build_chat_params(
342:         instructions: @instructions,
343:         messages: array_of_message_hashes,
344:         tools: @tools,
345:         tool_choice: tool_choice,
346:         parallel_tool_calls: parallel_tool_calls
347:       )
348:       @llm.chat(**params, &@block)
349:     end
350: 
351:     # Run the tools automatically
352:     #
353:     # @param tool_calls [Array<Hash>] The tool calls to run
354:     def run_tools(tool_calls)
355:       # Iterate over each function invocation and submit tool output
356:       tool_calls.each do |tool_call|
357:         run_tool(tool_call)
358:       end
359:     end
360: 
361:     # Run the tool call
362:     #
363:     # @param tool_call [Hash] The tool call to run
364:     # @return [Object] The result of the tool call
365:     def run_tool(tool_call)
366:       tool_call_id, tool_name, method_name, tool_arguments = @llm_adapter.extract_tool_call_args(tool_call: tool_call)
367: 
368:       tool_instance = tools.find do |t|
369:         t.class.tool_name == tool_name
370:       end or raise ArgumentError, "Tool: #{tool_name} not found in assistant.tools"
371: 
372:       # Call the callback if set
373:       tool_execution_callback.call(tool_call_id, tool_name, method_name, tool_arguments) if tool_execution_callback # rubocop:disable Style/SafeNavigation
374: 
375:       output = tool_instance.public_send(method_name, **tool_arguments)
376: 
377:       # Handle both ToolResponse and legacy return values
378:       if output.is_a?(ToolResponse)
379:         add_message(role: @llm_adapter.tool_role, content: output.content, image_url: output.image_url, tool_call_id: tool_call_id)
380:       else
381:         submit_tool_output(tool_call_id: tool_call_id, output: output)
382:       end
383:     end
384: 
385:     # Build a message
386:     #
387:     # @param role [String] The role of the message
388:     # @param content [String] The content of the message
389:     # @param image_url [String] The URL of the image to include in the message
390:     # @param tool_calls [Array<Hash>] The tool calls to include in the message
391:     # @param tool_call_id [String] The ID of the tool call to include in the message
392:     # @return [Langchain::Message] The Message object
393:     def build_message(role:, content: nil, image_url: nil, tool_calls: [], tool_call_id: nil)
394:       @llm_adapter.build_message(role: role, content: content, image_url: image_url, tool_calls: tool_calls, tool_call_id: tool_call_id)
395:     end
396: 
397:     # Increment the tokens count based on the last interaction with the LLM
398:     #
399:     # @param prompt_tokens [Integer] The number of used prmopt tokens
400:     # @param completion_tokens [Integer] The number of used completion tokens
401:     # @param total_tokens [Integer] The total number of used tokens
402:     # @return [Integer] The current total tokens count
403:     def record_used_tokens(prompt_tokens, completion_tokens, total_tokens_from_operation)
404:       @total_prompt_tokens += prompt_tokens if prompt_tokens
405:       @total_completion_tokens += completion_tokens if completion_tokens
406:       @total_tokens += total_tokens_from_operation if total_tokens_from_operation
407:     end
408: 
409:     def available_tool_names
410:       llm_adapter.available_tool_names(tools)
411:     end
412: 
413:     def validate_callback!(attr_name, callback)
414:       if !callback.nil? && !callback.respond_to?(:call)
415:         raise ArgumentError, "#{attr_name} must be a callable object, like Proc or lambda"
416:       end
417: 
418:       true
419:     end
420:   end
421: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Langchain
4:   class Assistant
5:     module LLM
6:       module Adapters
7:         class OpenAI < Base
8:           # Build the chat parameters for the OpenAI LLM
9:           #
10:           # @param messages [Array] The messages
11:           # @param instructions [String] The system instructions
12:           # @param tools [Array] The tools to use
13:           # @param tool_choice [String] The tool choice
14:           # @param parallel_tool_calls [Boolean] Whether to make parallel tool calls
15:           # @return [Hash] The chat parameters
16:           def build_chat_params(
17:             messages:,
18:             instructions:,
19:             tools:,
20:             tool_choice:,
21:             parallel_tool_calls:
22:           )
23:             params = {messages: messages}
24:             if tools.any?
25:               params[:tools] = build_tools(tools)
26:               params[:tool_choice] = build_tool_choice(tool_choice)
27:               # Temporary fix because OpenAI o1/o3/reasoning models don't support `parallel_tool_calls` parameter.
28:               # Set `Assistant.new(parallel_tool_calls: nil, ...)` to avoid the error.
29:               params[:parallel_tool_calls] = parallel_tool_calls unless parallel_tool_calls.nil?
30:             end
31:             params
32:           end
33: 
34:           # Build a OpenAI message
35:           #
36:           # @param role [String] The role of the message
37:           # @param content [String] The content of the message
38:           # @param image_url [String] The image URL
39:           # @param tool_calls [Array] The tool calls
40:           # @param tool_call_id [String] The tool call ID
41:           # @return [Messages::OpenAIMessage] The OpenAI message
42:           def build_message(role:, content: nil, image_url: nil, tool_calls: [], tool_call_id: nil)
43:             Messages::OpenAIMessage.new(role: role, content: content, image_url: image_url, tool_calls: tool_calls, tool_call_id: tool_call_id)
44:           end
45: 
46:           # Extract the tool call information from the OpenAI tool call hash
47:           #
48:           # @param tool_call [Hash] The tool call hash
49:           # @return [Array] The tool call information
50:           def extract_tool_call_args(tool_call:)
51:             tool_call_id = tool_call.dig("id")
52: 
53:             function_name = tool_call.dig("function", "name")
54:             tool_name, method_name = function_name.split("__")
55: 
56:             tool_arguments = tool_call.dig("function", "arguments")
57:             tool_arguments = if tool_arguments.is_a?(Hash)
58:               Langchain::Utils::HashTransformer.symbolize_keys(tool_arguments)
59:             else
60:               JSON.parse(tool_arguments, symbolize_names: true)
61:             end
62: 
63:             [tool_call_id, tool_name, method_name, tool_arguments]
64:           end
65: 
66:           # Build the tools for the OpenAI LLM
67:           def build_tools(tools)
68:             tools.map { |tool| tool.class.function_schemas.to_openai_format }.flatten
69:           end
70: 
71:           # Get the allowed assistant.tool_choice values for OpenAI
72:           def allowed_tool_choices
73:             ["auto", "none"]
74:           end
75: 
76:           # Get the available tool names for OpenAI
77:           def available_tool_names(tools)
78:             build_tools(tools).map { |tool| tool.dig(:function, :name) }
79:           end
80: 
81:           def tool_role
82:             Messages::OpenAIMessage::TOOL_ROLE
83:           end
84: 
85:           def support_system_message?
86:             Messages::OpenAIMessage::ROLES.include?("system")
87:           end
88: 
89:           private
90: 
91:           def build_tool_choice(choice)
92:             case choice
93:             when "auto"
94:               choice
95:             else
96:               {"type" => "function", "function" => {"name" => choice}}
97:             end
98:           end
99:         end
100:       end
101:     end
102:   end
103: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Langchain
4:   class Assistant
5:     module Messages
6:       class OpenAIMessage < Base
7:         # OpenAI uses the following roles:
8:         ROLES = [
9:           "system",
10:           "assistant",
11:           "user",
12:           "tool"
13:         ].freeze
14: 
15:         TOOL_ROLE = "tool"
16: 
17:         # Initialize a new OpenAI message
18:         #
19:         # @param role [String] The role of the message
20:         # @param content [String] The content of the message
21:         # @param image_url [String] The URL of the image
22:         # @param tool_calls [Array<Hash>] The tool calls made in the message
23:         # @param tool_call_id [String] The ID of the tool call
24:         def initialize(
25:           role:,
26:           content: nil,
27:           image_url: nil,
28:           tool_calls: [],
29:           tool_call_id: nil
30:         )
31:           raise ArgumentError, "Role must be one of #{ROLES.join(", ")}" unless ROLES.include?(role)
32:           raise ArgumentError, "Tool calls must be an array of hashes" unless tool_calls.is_a?(Array) && tool_calls.all? { |tool_call| tool_call.is_a?(Hash) }
33: 
34:           @role = role
35:           # Some Tools return content as a JSON hence `.to_s`
36:           @content = content.to_s
37:           @image_url = image_url
38:           @tool_calls = tool_calls
39:           @tool_call_id = tool_call_id
40:         end
41: 
42:         # Check if the message came from an LLM
43:         #
44:         # @return [Boolean] true/false whether this message was produced by an LLM
45:         def llm?
46:           assistant?
47:         end
48: 
49:         # Convert the message to an OpenAI API-compatible hash
50:         #
51:         # @return [Hash] The message as an OpenAI API-compatible hash
52:         def to_hash
53:           if assistant?
54:             assistant_hash
55:           elsif system?
56:             system_hash
57:           elsif tool?
58:             tool_hash
59:           elsif user?
60:             user_hash
61:           end
62:         end
63: 
64:         # Check if the message came from an LLM
65:         #
66:         # @return [Boolean] true/false whether this message was produced by an LLM
67:         def assistant?
68:           role == "assistant"
69:         end
70: 
71:         # Check if the message are system instructions
72:         #
73:         # @return [Boolean] true/false whether this message are system instructions
74:         def system?
75:           role == "system"
76:         end
77: 
78:         # Check if the message is a tool call
79:         #
80:         # @return [Boolean] true/false whether this message is a tool call
81:         def tool?
82:           role == "tool"
83:         end
84: 
85:         def user?
86:           role == "user"
87:         end
88: 
89:         # Convert the message to an OpenAI API-compatible hash
90:         # @return [Hash] The message as an OpenAI API-compatible hash, with the role as "assistant"
91:         def assistant_hash
92:           if tool_calls.any?
93:             {
94:               role: "assistant",
95:               tool_calls: tool_calls
96:             }
97:           else
98:             {
99:               role: "assistant",
100:               content: build_content_array
101:             }
102:           end
103:         end
104: 
105:         # Convert the message to an OpenAI API-compatible hash
106:         # @return [Hash] The message as an OpenAI API-compatible hash, with the role as "system"
107:         def system_hash
108:           {
109:             role: "system",
110:             content: build_content_array
111:           }
112:         end
113: 
114:         # Convert the message to an OpenAI API-compatible hash
115:         # @return [Hash] The message as an OpenAI API-compatible hash, with the role as "tool"
116:         def tool_hash
117:           {
118:             role: "tool",
119:             tool_call_id: tool_call_id,
120:             content: build_content_array
121:           }
122:         end
123: 
124:         # Convert the message to an OpenAI API-compatible hash
125:         # @return [Hash] The message as an OpenAI API-compatible hash, with the role as "user"
126:         def user_hash
127:           {
128:             role: "user",
129:             content: build_content_array
130:           }
131:         end
132: 
133:         # Builds the content value for the message hash
134:         # @return [Array<Hash>] An array of content hashes, with keys :type and :text or :image_url.
135:         def build_content_array
136:           content_details = []
137:           if content && !content.empty?
138:             content_details << {
139:               type: "text",
140:               text: content
141:             }
142:           end
143: 
144:           if image_url
145:             content_details << {
146:               type: "image_url",
147:               image_url: {
148:                 url: image_url
149:               }
150:             }
151:           end
152:           content_details
153:         end
154:       end
155:     end
156:   end
157: end

3: module Langchain
4:   class Assistant
5:     module LLM
6:       module Adapters
7:         class Anthropic < Base
8:           # Build the chat parameters for the Anthropic API
9:           #
10:           # @param messages [Array<Hash>] The messages
11:           # @param instructions [String] The system instructions
12:           # @param tools [Array<Hash>] The tools to use
13:           # @param tool_choice [String] The tool choice
14:           # @param parallel_tool_calls [Boolean] Whether to make parallel tool calls
15:           # @return [Hash] The chat parameters
16:           def build_chat_params(
17:             messages:,
18:             instructions:,
19:             tools:,
20:             tool_choice:,
21:             parallel_tool_calls:
22:           )
23:             params = {messages: messages}
24:             if tools.any?
25:               params[:tools] = build_tools(tools)
26:               params[:tool_choice] = build_tool_choice(tool_choice, parallel_tool_calls)
27:             end
28:             params[:system] = instructions if instructions
29:             params
30:           end
31: 
32:           # Build an Anthropic message
33:           #
34:           # @param role [String] The role of the message
35:           # @param content [String] The content of the message
36:           # @param image_url [String] The image URL
37:           # @param tool_calls [Array<Hash>] The tool calls
38:           # @param tool_call_id [String] The tool call ID
39:           # @return [Messages::AnthropicMessage] The Anthropic message
40:           def build_message(role:, content: nil, image_url: nil, tool_calls: [], tool_call_id: nil)
41:             Messages::AnthropicMessage.new(role: role, content: content, image_url: image_url, tool_calls: tool_calls, tool_call_id: tool_call_id)
42:           end
43: 
44:           # Extract the tool call information from the Anthropic tool call hash
45:           #
46:           # @param tool_call [Hash] The tool call hash, format: {"type"=>"tool_use", "id"=>"toolu_01TjusbFApEbwKPRWTRwzadR", "name"=>"news_retriever__get_top_headlines", "input"=>{"country"=>"us", "page_size"=>10}}], "stop_reason"=>"tool_use"}
47:           # @return [Array] The tool call information
48:           def extract_tool_call_args(tool_call:)
49:             tool_call_id = tool_call.dig(:id)
50:             function_name = tool_call.dig(:name)
51:             tool_name, method_name = function_name.split("__")
52:             tool_arguments = tool_call.dig(:input).transform_keys(&:to_sym)
53:             [tool_call_id, tool_name, method_name, tool_arguments]
54:           end
55: 
56:           # Build the tools for the Anthropic API
57:           def build_tools(tools)
58:             tools.map { |tool| tool.class.function_schemas.to_anthropic_format }.flatten
59:           end
60: 
61:           # Get the allowed assistant.tool_choice values for Anthropic
62:           def allowed_tool_choices
63:             ["auto", "any"]
64:           end
65: 
66:           # Get the available tool function names for Anthropic
67:           #
68:           # @param tools [Array<Hash>] The tools
69:           # @return [Array<String>] The tool function names
70:           def available_tool_names(tools)
71:             build_tools(tools).map { |tool| tool.dig(:name) }
72:           end

3: module Langchain
4:   class Assistant
5:     module LLM
6:       module Adapters
7:         class GoogleGemini < Base
8:           # Build the chat parameters for the Google Gemini LLM
9:           #
10:           # @param messages [Array] The messages
11:           # @param instructions [String] The system instructions
12:           # @param tools [Array] The tools to use
13:           # @param tool_choice [String] The tool choice
14:           # @param parallel_tool_calls [Boolean] Whether to make parallel tool calls
15:           # @return [Hash] The chat parameters
16:           def build_chat_params(
17:             messages:,
18:             instructions:,
19:             tools:,
20:             tool_choice:,
21:             parallel_tool_calls:
22:           )
23:             Langchain.logger.warn "WARNING: `parallel_tool_calls:` is not supported by Google Gemini currently" if parallel_tool_calls
24: 
25:             params = {messages: messages}
26:             if tools.any?
27:               params[:tools] = build_tools(tools)
28:               params[:system] = instructions if instructions
29:               params[:tool_choice] = build_tool_config(tool_choice)
30:             end
31:             params
32:           end
33: 
34:           # Build a Google Gemini message
35:           #
36:           # @param role [String] The role of the message
37:           # @param content [String] The content of the message
38:           # @param image_url [String] The image URL
39:           # @param tool_calls [Array] The tool calls
40:           # @param tool_call_id [String] The tool call ID
41:           # @return [Messages::GoogleGeminiMessage] The Google Gemini message
42:           def build_message(role:, content: nil, image_url: nil, tool_calls: [], tool_call_id: nil)
43:             Langchain.logger.warn "Image URL is not supported by Google Gemini" if image_url
44: 
45:             Messages::GoogleGeminiMessage.new(role: role, content: content, tool_calls: tool_calls, tool_call_id: tool_call_id)
46:           end
47: 
48:           # Extract the tool call information from the Google Gemini tool call hash
49:           #
50:           # @param tool_call [Hash] The tool call hash, format: {"functionCall"=>{"name"=>"weather__execute", "args"=>{"input"=>"NYC"}}}
51:           # @return [Array] The tool call information
52:           def extract_tool_call_args(tool_call:)
53:             tool_call_id = tool_call.dig("functionCall", "name")
54:             function_name = tool_call.dig("functionCall", "name")
55:             tool_name, method_name = function_name.split("__")
56:             tool_arguments = tool_call.dig("functionCall", "args").transform_keys(&:to_sym)
57:             [tool_call_id, tool_name, method_name, tool_arguments]
58:           end
59: 
60:           # Build the tools for the Google Gemini LLM
61:           #
62:           # @param tools [Array<Langchain::Tool::Base>] The tools
63:           # @return [Array] The tools in Google Gemini format
64:           def build_tools(tools)
65:             tools.map { |tool| tool.class.function_schemas.to_google_gemini_format }.flatten
66:           end
67: 
68:           # Get the allowed assistant.tool_choice values for Google Gemini
69:           def allowed_tool_choices
70:             ["auto", "none"]
71:           end
72: 
73:           # Get the available tool names for Google Gemini
74:           def available_tool_names(tools)
75:             build_tools(tools).map { |tool| tool.dig(:name) }
76:           end
77: 
78:           def tool_role
79:             Messages::GoogleGeminiMessage::TOOL_ROLE
80:           end
81: 
82:           def support_system_message?
83:             Messages::GoogleGeminiMessage::ROLES.include?("system")
84:           end
85: 
86:           private
87: 
88:           def build_tool_config(choice)
89:             case choice
90:             when "auto"
91:               {function_calling_config: {mode: "auto"}}
92:             when "none"
93:               {function_calling_config: {mode: "none"}}
94:             else
95:               {function_calling_config: {mode: "any", allowed_function_names: [choice]}}
96:             end
97:           end
98:         end
99:       end
100:     end
101:   end
102: end

### Step 3: Map the provider-integration family and its contract

Shared contract (from `LLM::Adapters::Base` `lib/langchain/assistant/llm/adapters/base.rb`):

| Method | Purpose | Line |
|---|---|---|
| `build_chat_params` | Format chat request hash for provider | `lib/langchain/assistant/llm/adapters/base.rb:16` |
| `extract_tool_call_args` | Parse provider tool-call result into `[id, tool_name, method_name, args]` | `lib/langchain/assistant/llm/adapters/base.rb:30` |
| `build_message` | Construct provider-specific message object | `lib/langchain/assistant/llm/adapters/base.rb:42` |
| `support_system_message?` | Declare whether provider supports `role: "system"` | `lib/langchain/assistant/llm/adapters/base.rb:49` |
| `tool_role` | Return role string for tool-output messages | `lib/langchain/assistant/llm/adapters/base.rb:56` |

Plus de facto required helper methods used by the assistant:
- `build_tools(tools)` — serialize tools to provider schema
- `allowed_tool_choices` — valid `tool_choice` values
- `available_tool_names(tools)` — tool names for validation

Provider customizations:

| Adapter | File | Custom behavior |
|---|---|---|
| OpenAI | `lib/langchain/assistant/llm/adapters/openai.rb` | OpenAI-style `tools`/`tool_choice`/`parallel_tool_calls`; tool calls shaped `{"id", "function": {"name", "arguments"}}`; `tool_choice` supports `"auto"` + function name; uses `function_schemas.to_openai_format` `openai.rb:68` |
| Anthropic | `lib/langchain/assistant/llm/adapters/anthropic.rb` | System prompt passed as top-level `:system`; tool-choice supports `"auto"`/`"any"` + named tool; tool calls shaped `{:id, :name, :input}`; uses `function_schemas.to_anthropic_format` `anthropic.rb:58` |
| GoogleGemini | `lib/langchain/assistant/llm/adapters/google_gemini.rb` | No `parallel_tool_calls`; no image URL; tool config uses `function_calling_config`; tool calls shaped `{"functionCall": {"name", "args"}}`; `tool_call_id` is function name; uses `function_schemas.to_google_gemini_format` `google_gemini.rb:65` |
| MistralAI | `lib/langchain/assistant/llm/adapters/mistralai.rb` | No `parallel_tool_calls`; OpenAI-compatible tool/response format; uses `function_schemas.to_openai_format` `mistralai.rb:67` |
| Ollama | `lib/langchain/assistant/llm/adapters/ollama.rb` | No `parallel_tool_calls`, no `tool_choice`; OpenAI-compatible tool/response format; uses `function_schemas.to_openai_format` `ollama.rb:86` |
| AwsBedrockAnthropic | `lib/langchain/assistant/llm/adapters/aws_bedrock_anthropic.rb` | Inherits Anthropic; overrides `build_tool_choice` to drop `disable_parallel_tool_use` because Bedrock doesn't support it `aws_bedrock_anthropic.rb:13` |

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Langchain
4:   class Assistant
5:     module Messages
6:       class AnthropicMessage < Base
7:         ROLES = [
8:           "assistant",
9:           "user",
10:           "tool_result"
11:         ].freeze
12: 
13:         TOOL_ROLE = "tool_result"
14: 
15:         # Initialize a new Anthropic message
16:         #
17:         # @param role [String] The role of the message
18:         # @param content [String] The content of the message
19:         # @param tool_calls [Array<Hash>] The tool calls made in the message
20:         # @param tool_call_id [String] The ID of the tool call
21:         def initialize(
22:           role:,
23:           content: nil,
24:           image_url: nil,
25:           tool_calls: [],
26:           tool_call_id: nil
27:         )
28:           raise ArgumentError, "Role must be one of #{ROLES.join(", ")}" unless ROLES.include?(role)
29:           raise ArgumentError, "Tool calls must be an array of hashes" unless tool_calls.is_a?(Array) && tool_calls.all? { |tool_call| tool_call.is_a?(Hash) }
30: 
31:           @role = role
32:           # Some Tools return content as a JSON hence `.to_s`
33:           @content = content.to_s
34:           @image_url = image_url
35:           @tool_calls = tool_calls
36:           @tool_call_id = tool_call_id
37:         end
38: 
39:         # Convert the message to an Anthropic API-compatible hash
40:         #
41:         # @return [Hash] The message as an Anthropic API-compatible hash
42:         def to_hash
43:           if assistant?
44:             assistant_hash
45:           elsif tool?
46:             tool_hash
47:           elsif user?
48:             user_hash
49:           end
50:         end
51: 
52:         # Convert the message to an Anthropic API-compatible hash
53:         #
54:         # @return [Hash] The message as an Anthropic API-compatible hash, with the role as "assistant"
55:         def assistant_hash
56:           content_array = []
57:           if content && !content.empty?
58:             content_array << {type: "text", text: content}
59:           end
60: 
61:           {
62:             role: "assistant",
63:             content: content_array.concat(tool_calls)
64:           }
65:         end
66: 
67:         # Convert the message to an Anthropic API-compatible hash
68:         #
69:         # @return [Hash] The message as an Anthropic API-compatible hash, with the role as "user"
70:         def tool_hash
71:           {
72:             role: "user",
73:             # TODO: Tool can also return images
74:             # https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/build-with-claude/tool-use#handling-tool-use-and-tool-result-content-blocks
75:             content: [
76:               {
77:                 type: "tool_result",
78:                 tool_use_id: tool_call_id,
79:                 content: content
80:               }
81:             ]
82:           }
83:         end
84: 
85:         # Convert the message to an Anthropic API-compatible hash
86:         #
87:         # @return [Hash] The message as an Anthropic API-compatible hash, with the role as "user"
88:         def user_hash
89:           {
90:             role: "user",
91:             content: build_content_array
92:           }
93:         end
94: 
95:         # Builds the content value for the message hash
96:         # @return [Array<Hash>] An array of content hashes
97:         def build_content_array
98:           content_details = []
99: 
100:           if content && !content.empty?
101:             content_details << {
102:               type: "text",
103:               text: content
104:             }
105:           end
106: 
107:           if image
108:             content_details << {
109:               type: "image",

17:   class OpenAI < Base
18:     DEFAULTS = {
19:       n: 1,
20:       chat_model: "gpt-4o-mini",
21:       embedding_model: "text-embedding-3-small"
22:     }.freeze
23: 
24:     EMBEDDING_SIZES = {
25:       "text-embedding-ada-002" => 1536,
26:       "text-embedding-3-large" => 3072,
27:       "text-embedding-3-small" => 1536
28:     }.freeze

30:     # Initialize an OpenAI LLM instance
31:     #
32:     # @param api_key [String] The API key to use
33:     # @param client_options [Hash] Options to pass to the OpenAI::Client constructor
34:     def initialize(api_key:, llm_options: {}, default_options: {})

37:       llm_options[:log_errors] = Langchain.logger.debug? unless llm_options.key?(:log_errors)
38: 
39:       @client = ::OpenAI::Client.new(access_token: api_key, **llm_options) do |f|

43:       @defaults = DEFAULTS.merge(default_options)

46:         logprobs: {},
47:         top_logprobs: {},
48:         n: {default: @defaults[:n]},
49:         temperature: {default: @defaults[:temperature]},
50:         user: {},
51:         response_format: {default: @defaults[:response_format]}
52:       )
53:       chat_parameters.ignore(:top_k)

56:     # Generate an embedding for a given text
57:     #
58:     # @param text [String] The text to generate an embedding for
59:     # @param model [String] ID of the model to use
60:     # @param encoding_format [String] The format to return the embeddings in. Can be either float or base64.
61:     # @param user [String] A unique identifier representing your end-user
62:     # @return [Langchain::LLM::Response::OpenAIResponse] Response object
63:     def embed(
64:       text:,
65:       model: defaults[:embedding_model],
66:       encoding_format: nil,
67:       user: nil,
68:       dimensions: @defaults[:dimensions]
69:     )
70:       raise ArgumentError.new("text argument is required") if text.empty?
71:       raise ArgumentError.new("model argument is required") if model.empty?
72:       raise ArgumentError.new("encoding_format must be either float or base64") if encoding_format && %w[float base64].include?(encoding_format)
73: 
74:       parameters = {
75:         input: text,
76:         model: model
77:       }
78:       parameters[:encoding_format] = encoding_format if encoding_format
79:       parameters[:user] = user if user
80: 
81:       if dimensions
82:         parameters[:dimensions] = dimensions
83:       elsif EMBEDDING_SIZES.key?(model)
84:         parameters[:dimensions] = EMBEDDING_SIZES[model]
85:       end

88:       parameters.delete(:dimensions) if model == "text-embedding-ada-002"

90:       response = with_api_error_handling do
91:         client.embeddings(parameters: parameters)
92:       end

94:       Langchain::LLM::Response::OpenAIResponse.new(response)
95:     end

97:     # rubocop:disable Style/ArgumentsForwarding
98:     # Generate a completion for a given prompt
99:     #
100:     # @param prompt [String] The prompt to generate a completion for
101:     # @param params [Hash] The parameters to pass to the `chat()` method
102:     # @return [Langchain::LLM::Response::OpenAIResponse] Response object
103:     # @deprecated Use {chat} instead.
104:     def complete(prompt:, **params)
105:       Langchain.logger.warn "DEPRECATED: `Langchain::LLM::OpenAI#complete` is deprecated, and will be removed in the next major version. Use `Langchain::LLM::OpenAI#chat` instead."
106: 
107:       if params[:stop_sequences]
108:         params[:stop] = params.delete(:stop_sequences)
109:       end
110:       # Should we still accept the `messages: []` parameter here?
111:       messages = [{role: "user", content: prompt}]
112:       chat(messages: messages, **params)
113:     end
114: 
115:     # rubocop:enable Style/ArgumentsForwarding
116: 
117:     # Generate a chat completion for given messages.
118:     #
119:     # @param [Hash] params unified chat parmeters from [Langchain::LLM::Parameters::Chat::SCHEMA]
120:     # @option params [Array<Hash>] :messages List of messages comprising the conversation so far
121:     # @option params [String] :model ID of the model to use
122:     def chat(params = {}, &block)
123:       parameters = chat_parameters.to_params(params)
124: 
125:       raise ArgumentError.new("messages argument is required") if Array(parameters[:messages]).empty?
126:       raise ArgumentError.new("model argument is required") if parameters[:model].to_s.empty?
127:       if parameters[:tool_choice] && Array(parameters[:tools]).empty?
128:         raise ArgumentError.new("'tool_choice' is only allowed when 'tools' are specified.")
129:       end
130: 
131:       if block
132:         @response_chunks = []
133:         parameters[:stream_options] = {include_usage: true}
134:         parameters[:stream] = proc do |chunk, _bytesize|
135:           chunk_content = chunk.dig("choices", 0) || {}
136:           @response_chunks << chunk
137:           yield chunk_content
138:         end
139:       end
140: 
141:       response = with_api_error_handling do
142:         client.chat(parameters: parameters)
143:       end
144: 
145:       response = response_from_chunks if block
146:       reset_response_chunks
147: 
148:       Langchain::LLM::Response::OpenAIResponse.new(response)
149:     end
150: 
151:     # Generate a summary for a given text
152:     #
153:     # @param text [String] The text to generate a summary for
154:     # @return [String] The summary
155:     def summarize(text:)
156:       prompt_template = Langchain::Prompt.load_from_path(
157:         file_path: Langchain.root.join("langchain/llm/prompts/summarize_template.yaml")
158:       )
159:       prompt = prompt_template.format(text: text)
160: 
161:       complete(prompt: prompt)
162:     end
163: 
164:     def default_dimensions
165:       @defaults[:dimensions] || EMBEDDING_SIZES.fetch(defaults[:embedding_model])
166:     end
167: 
168:     private
169: 
170:     attr_reader :response_chunks
171: 
172:     def reset_response_chunks
173:       @response_chunks = []
174:     end
175: 
176:     def with_api_error_handling
177:       response = yield
178:       return if response.nil? || response.empty?
179: 
180:       raise Langchain::LLM::ApiError.new "OpenAI API error: #{response.dig("error", "message")}" if response&.dig("error")
181: 
182:       response
183:     end
184: 
185:     def response_from_chunks
186:       grouped_chunks = @response_chunks
187:         .group_by { |chunk| chunk.dig("choices", 0, "index") }
188:         .except(nil) # the last chunk (that contains the token usage) has no index
189:       final_choices = grouped_chunks.map do |index, chunks|
190:         {
191:           "index" => index,
192:           "message" => {
193:             "role" => "assistant",
194:             "content" => chunks.map { |chunk| chunk.dig("choices", 0, "delta", "content") }.join,
195:             "tool_calls" => tool_calls_from_choice_chunks(chunks)
196:           }.compact,
197:           "finish_reason" => chunks.last.dig("choices", 0, "finish_reason")
198:         }
199:       end
200:       @response_chunks.first&.slice("id", "object", "created", "model")&.merge({"choices" => final_choices, "usage" => @response_chunks.last["usage"]})
201:     end
202: 
203:     def tool_calls_from_choice_chunks(choice_chunks)
204:       tool_call_chunks = choice_chunks.select { |chunk| chunk.dig("choices", 0, "delta", "tool_calls") }
205:       return nil if tool_call_chunks.empty?
206: 
207:       tool_call_chunks.group_by { |chunk| chunk.dig("choices", 0, "delta", "tool_calls", 0, "index") }.map do |index, chunks|
208:         first_chunk = chunks.first
209: 
210:         {
211:           "id" => first_chunk.dig("choices", 0, "delta", "tool_calls", 0, "id"),
212:           "type" => first_chunk.dig("choices", 0, "delta", "tool_calls", 0, "type"),
213:           "function" => {
214:             "name" => first_chunk.dig("choices", 0, "delta", "tool_calls", 0, "function", "name"),
215:             "arguments" => chunks.map { |chunk| chunk.dig("choices", 0, "delta", "tool_calls", 0, "function", "arguments") }.join
216:           }
217:         }
218:       end
219:     end
